title: NOC 11100 – Financial Auditors and Accountants | GTR Immigration
description: Financial Auditors and Accountants classified under NOC 11100 belong to one of the most consistently in-demand professional groups in Canada’s immigration system. These specialists play a critical role in financial compliance, taxation, auditing, and corporate reporting—making them strong candidates for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs).

classification.teer: 1 – Occupations usually require a university degree

duties: Professionals in this occupation may perform some or all of the following:
Examine journal entries, ledgers, bank statements, inventories, expenditures, and tax returns
Ensure compliance with accounting standards and internal-control systems
Prepare audit reports and improvement recommendations
Conduct field audits for compliance with Canadian legislation
Supervise junior auditors or accounting staff
Prepare corporate and personal tax filings
Provide business and tax advisory services
Act as trustees in bankruptcy proceedings (when licensed)
Train articling students and junior professionals
employment_requirements: Typical licensing and education pathways include:
CPA (Chartered Professional Accountant) designation requiring a university degree, approved professional training, and supervised work experience
Provincial regulatory-body membership for public-practice accountants and auditors
Additional recognition for internal auditors from professional institutes
Special licensing to act as a trustee in bankruptcy
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is expected to create 88,000 new roles for Financial Auditors and Accountants, while approximately 79,800 job seekers may enter the field. For internationally trained professionals—especially applicants from India—this represents strong long-term opportunities when supported by a targeted immigration plan.
labour_market_demand: Financial Auditors and Accountants are in sustained demand across Canada’s banking, accounting, investment, and corporate-finance sectors. Hiring expanded following the pandemic and is expected to remain strong over the coming decade.
Excellent immigration opportunities for internationally trained professionals
Annual earnings typically range from $44,300 to $120,300, depending on experience, province, and specialization.
Why Financial Auditors & Accountants Choose Canada
Professionals immigrating under NOC 11100 benefit from:
Strong labour protections and work-life balance
Clear pathways to Canadian Permanent Residence (PR)
